title: InsightKit Common Definitions
description: Shared building blocks for every InsightKit dataset. The core idea is the confidence-wrapped field: no raw scalar is ever stored bare. Every collected value is wrapped in an object carrying its value, a confidence score in [0,1], an explicit `unknown` flag, and provenance. Downstream phases (knowledge graph, visualizations, report) MUST treat any field with `unknown: true` as missing data and SHOULD weight or annotate rendered values by `confidence`.

confidence.title: Confidence Score
confidence.description: How much the collector trusts this value. 0 = pure guess / no evidence, 1 = directly stated by an authoritative first-party source. When `unknown` is true, confidence MUST be 0.

sourceType.title: Source Type
sourceType.description: Where the value came from, used to reason about trust.

provenance.title: Provenance
provenance.description: Optional evidence trail for an individual field. Allows a reviewer to audit how a value was obtained.

source.description: Human-readable origin, typically a URL or file path.

as_of.description: ISO-8601 date the value was observed/collected.

method.description: Short note on how the value was derived (e.g. 'parsed package.json', 'inferred from hero copy', 'GitHub REST /repos').

baseField.title: Base Confidence Field
baseField.description: Abstract shape every typed field extends. Concrete fields override `value`. Required keys are always present so consumers can rely on the contract without existence checks.

unknown.description: True when the value could not be determined. When true, `value` MUST be null and `confidence` MUST be 0.

source.description: Convenience pointer to the primary source (URL or path). Use `provenance` for richer detail.

stringField.title: String Field
stringField.description: Confidence-wrapped string. `value` is null when unknown.

urlField.title: URL Field
urlField.description: Confidence-wrapped URL. `value` is null when unknown.

numberField.title: Number Field
numberField.description: Confidence-wrapped number (may be an estimate; use `confidence` to express precision).

booleanField.title: Boolean Field
booleanField.description: Confidence-wrapped tri-state boolean. `value` null + `unknown` true means 'not determined'; this is distinct from a confident false.

enumField.title: Enum Field
enumField.description: Confidence-wrapped categorical value. The set of allowed strings is constrained at each use site via an additional allOf in the consuming schema; the literal string 'unknown' is always permitted as a fallback in addition to setting `unknown: true`.

stringArrayField.title: String Array Field
stringArrayField.description: Confidence-wrapped list of strings (features, integrations, investors). When unknown, `value` is null (not []), so an empty list confidently means 'none' while null means 'not determined'.

metricField.title: Metric Field
metricField.description: Confidence-wrapped numeric metric that may be exact or estimated, with an optional range and unit. Used for soft figures like employee count, ARR, or follower counts.

datasetMeta.title: Dataset Metadata
datasetMeta.description: Standard envelope metadata present on every top-level dataset.

schema_version.description: SemVer of the InsightKit data contract this file conforms to.

dataset.description: Identifier of the dataset, equal to the file stem.

generated_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the dataset was produced.

entityRef.title: Entity Reference
entityRef.description: Stable join key linking per-competitor records across datasets. Equal to a competitor `id` from competitors.json, or the reserved literal 'self' for the analyzed product.
